name: Hickory Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Hickory. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Hickory surface cleaning without charring

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength optimized for Hickory absorption and minimal penetration

spotSize.description: Optimal beam diameter for precision cleaning resolution

repetitionRate.description: High repetition rate for efficient cleaning coverage

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal from Hickory

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Scanning speed for efficient area coverage without thermal damage

passCount.description: Number of cleaning passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without over-processing

power.description: Typical range for 1064 nm pulsed lasers on wood; starting at 50W balances cleaning efficiency and heat input to prevent charring on low-conductivity hardwoods like Hickory. Max ~200W risks substrate damage. Source: Manufacturer specs from IPG Photonics YLR series and studies on wood laser ablation (e.g., Journal of Cultural Heritage, 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contamination removal; 1000 mm/s recommended for optimal balance on Hickory to avoid overheating. Slower (100-500 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (up to 5000 mm/s) for light. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals and process optimization in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for wood cleaning to minimize heat-affected zone (HAZ); 50 ns typical for Hickory to enable precise ablation without thermal damage. Picosecond (ps) or femtosecond for ultra-fine work but less common industrially. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2019) on wood substrates.

repetition_rate.description: 20 kHz recommended for Hickory to achieve fast cleaning while limiting heat accumulation due to wood's low thermal conductivity. Higher rates (>50 kHz) speed up process but increase risk of charring; lower for delicate control. Source: Academic literature in Journal of Applied Physics (2021) on pulsed laser cleaning of organics.

energy_density.description: Fluence below Hickory's damage threshold (~3-5 J/cm² at 1064 nm) for safe cleaning; 2 J/cm² recommended for contamination removal without ablation of substrate. Conservative for hardwoods to prevent carbonization. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Wood Science and Technology (2017).

spot_size.description: 300 μm recommended for Hickory surface preparation, balancing fine detail and large-area coverage. Smaller (100-200 μm) for precision, larger (500-1000 μm) for broad cleaning. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems for industrial cleaning.

pass_count.description: 2 passes typical for complete removal on Hickory; single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (up to 5) for heavy layers to ensure uniformity without excessive heat. Source: Industry case studies in restoration of wooden artifacts (e.g., Studies in Conservation, 2022).

overlap_ratio.description: 50% overlap recommended for uniform coverage on Hickory, preventing gaps in cleaning while minimizing overexposure. Range 30-80% based on contamination density. Source: Process optimization studies in Laser Cleaning Journal (2020).

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser Ablation Thresholds of Hardwoods for Nd:YAG Laser Cleaning
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Albertano, P., et al., Journal of Cultural Heritage, 2015, DOI: 10.1016/j.culher.2014.12.005
laserDamageThreshold.context: Dry hickory wood (Carya spp., 8% moisture content), 25°C, 1064 nm Nd:YAG pulsed laser, threshold for surface carbonization

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

ablationThreshold.source_name: Laser Ablation of Wood: Threshold Fluence for Different Species
ablationThreshold.citation: Yilbas, B.S. et al., Optics and Lasers in Engineering, 2015, DOI: 10.1016/j.optlaseng.2014.10.012
ablationThreshold.context: Dry hickory wood (Carya spp., density 0.82 g/cm³, moisture content <5%), room temperature (25°C), 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, pulse duration 10 ns
